I can't see much of the box, but in the dimly lit bar, it seems like there are only a few pairs of men and women besides me.
If you're not sitting at the counter in a place like this, it either means you love solitude a lot or you're up to something shady.
The bar isn't very decorated. With it being this dark, I guess there aren't many people who want to strain their eyes to see things.
Thanks to its extremely plain appearance, loud and foolish youths don’t ruin the atmosphere of the place. That's about the only good thing about this bar.
He insists he's not gay, but with a face like that, guys of that sort wouldn't ignore him, huh?
He might have a past he doesn't want to touch on, so it's probably best not to dig too deep.
The bartender's uniform suits him well. Does he have long experience in this line of work? He doesn’t look that old.
With his sharp eyes and cool demeanor, there are times I even feel fear. What kind of career has he built in a past I don't know?
I'll do my best to get the requested item as quickly as possible. I’ll contact you once it arrives.
But really, what do you even use something like that for?
Actually, you see, I got a scalpel from a strange place, and I thought it might be related to that incident.
I think that’s quite a leap in logic, but you must have some basis for it, right?
It’s a scalpel obtained from a person who apparently last met that woman involved in the search.
I see.
So, maybe that means?
Just between us, that woman has had her wrist severed.
The moment the target went from a pet to a person, we got tangled in something bloody.
Ah.
I remember now.
What is it?
Could you do me a favor?
Depends on what it is.
I want a Luminol reagent.
What would you even use something like that for?
Oh, if it’s too much, that’s fine.
There’s nothing impossible for me. Alright, I’ll take care of it.
Thanks,
No problem.
What’s wrong? You look tired.
Oh, it's been the same lately.
Yeah, I’m definitely tired today. I’ve had a rough morning.
Oh dear, I’m sorry to hear that. Should I get you something delicious?
Yeah, please.
Alright, it's squid ink tagliolini.
Is it done already? Ah, looks delicious. I love pasta.
Hehe, I feel a bit like a housewife.
M-mhm!
C-cut it out with those jokes.
I'd prefer to avoid that, too.
It’s easy to misunderstand, but I’m quite a normal person.
Ah, that statement is discriminatory against gay people.
I'm not discriminating at all.
But hey, why are we even talking about this?
What do you mean?
Isn't this something you brought up?
You always play dumb, and it's frustrating.
Honestly, I'm not gay.
Remember the female reporter from BAM I mentioned before?
Yeah. She seemed like a tough opponent.
Right. It looks like she's gotten involved in the case.
Oh! She's the victim from that ear incident that made the headlines this morning, right?
What?
I thought you might know something.
I'm not a god, you know.
It's not like I liked her or anything. But when something like this happens, it really gets to me.
It's normal to feel that way about someone you had any connection with.
That's true.
But, if both ears are cut off, they won't be able to hear anything.
Yeah. Even if she's alive, it's sad thinking about her future.
Don't dwell on it too much; for now, we just have to wait for good news.
By the way, do you know there’s a rumor that the serial killer is a failed doctor?
No. But where did that rumor come from?
Apparently, they're doing body dismemberment with just a scalpel, which requires some serious knowledge.
Isn't that something you could just learn from books?
True. I wonder what's going through the police's mind.
It seems like the criminal is far from getting caught.
Maybe the police are busy with various things? They seem to be all over the place.
Oh, are you defending the police now?
I never intended that. But, without a certain level of practical experience, it might be tough to dissect with just one scalpel.
Huh, something's bothering me.
I wonder when this case will finally come to an end.
Even if I don’t take care of it, you have the job of finding someone, right?
I guess being a detective just isn’t for me.
That was obvious from the start.
It's a bit late to be used by others, and I bought the license and opened an office, but I can't even do the job of locating people properly.
It's been less than two years since opening the office, and you don't even have the title of a former detective, so just think of it as training and do your best.
Training, huh.
Getting anxious and patching things up won’t work. Isn’t it better to take it one step at a time for now?
Did you get an autograph from Jennifer?
Well, you left before you could get it.
Oh, right, that’s true.
But she sure figured out this place well. Even Yuriko-chan doesn't really know for sure.
Isn't it easy for her to investigate?
Once you become that famous, most things can be done by someone else.
The question is, why did she come to this shop despite all that?
She said she wanted to see me, right?
That sounds suspicious.
Oh, so what? Are you saying it’s strange for her to say that to me?
No, not at all.
It’s just that the rumors about her aren’t all good.
I wouldn't say be wary, but it might be wise to be cautious until you understand her true intentions.
Hey, take a look at this.
It's a beautiful ring, isn't it?
You're joking. That's the item belonging to the woman I'm looking for.
You're talking about the woman with the wrist, right?
Yeah, her name is Ginny.
Even if you show me something like this, I wouldn't understand anything.
Well, I can at least tell it's quite expensive.
Is this really that expensive?
It might look cheap just from the design, but it's not some fashion ring you can find around.
The large ruby in the center and the surrounding pearls are of really high quality, this ring.
Oh.
As expected.
You really know your jewelry.
Normally, it's better to avoid carrying something like this.
About how much is it?
Well, I think it's at least 30,000 dollars, but I can't be exact.
What!? This is 30,000 dollars!? Ugh.
Hmm, jewelry is really deep.
It must have been something important to that woman, but it's strange.
Strange? This ring?
No, it's strange that the person who sent the left hand delivered such a valuable ring intact.
That's true.
I think she wouldn’t be recognizable without this.
Out of spite or to keep her quiet?
In any case, it's certain that the criminal isn't after valuables.
This incident might be unexpectedly deep.
If she's a political journalist, it's not surprising she was kidnapped, but she's just a second-rate journalist from a yellow paper.
Did she have some scoop that would require her to get kidnapped?
Should we go back to the Editorial Department in Baked Apple?
Right.
She was indeed an unpleasant woman. But she shouldn't have been unpleasant enough for someone to kidnap her.
Well, that can't be said. In this town, it's not strange to be hated enough to be killed by someone at any moment.
If I could forget, I would really like to.
Strong-willed women tend to be disliked. Especially if they're successful, it's even more so. There's no evidence to conclusively say she's the perpetrator.
As for the rumor about the assistant and a knife, who knows what's true or not? Gossip tends to embellish.
If Jennifer is aware that she's disliked and is using that as a cover, what then?
Even if everyone thinks she's suspicious, it’s because her reputation is bad.
If she really is...
Let’s hold off on excluding Jennifer from the suspect list for now.
It's not like she lacks education, and she doesn't seem to have had a bad upbringing. Why would she choose this line of work?
Though seeing her familiarity with the underworld, she doesn't seem like just any ordinary rich kid.
No matter who he is, it’s not my concern.
Besides, it's really inconvenient without him.
After all,
I have no impossibilities.
What a calm face to state that; I’ll make sure not to make an enemy of her.
Anyway, I can trust her with this.
Gruesome murders often have male perpetrators. In this case, the victims were all women.
It's only natural to think that some pervert is doing this to satisfy his abnormal sexual desires.
Targeting only prostitutes for a serial murder and sending dismembered body parts shows a craving for attention.
Jack the Ripper.
Isn't it?
But there are so many show-offs and failed doctors in New York, it's ridiculous.
This guy is.
It feels like this case is a bit too much of a burden for me.
I guess I shouldn't have become a detective after all.
If I had just become a lawyer or a doctor, I'd have a house in the suburbs by now.
But opportunities to chase murder cases are pretty rare. Most of the time it's just investigating a husband's infidelity.
Am I lucky?
To suddenly find myself chasing a murder case like this, I must have really bad luck.
That's me.
No, at first it was just a missing persons case.
Anyway, I've started this, so I have no choice. I need to earn enough to support myself and Yuriko.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
